mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate compress equity discount rates and stimulate corporate borrowing, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or forward returns are negative in 65% of rate-decline months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market deterioration signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings pressure, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 70% of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in real short-term interest rates and stable federal funds rates signal real yield compression that lowers equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses predictive power in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days during economic recovery phases.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in 65% of recovery-phase months with rising industrial production, or the mechanism fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly increases in real gross domestic product reflect expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, which consistently predict positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive GDP growth quarters coincide with negative 60-day equity returns in 70% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation accelerates beyond a 0.4% monthly threshold, anticipated central bank tightening and margin compression trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in 65% of months where CPI inflation exceeds the threshold, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 52% in validation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and industrial production confirms synchronized economic expansion, reducing recession risk and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The synchronized growth signal yields negative 5-day returns in 60% of instances, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in panel regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in housing starts signal weakening residential investment and broader demand contraction, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in 70% of late-cycle months with falling housing starts, or the housing signal fails to predict equity direction with a t-statistic above 1.5.
